## PR Title

<!-- Example: docs(learning): strengthen SRE and solutions-architecture teaching flow -->

## PR Type

- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Test
- [ ] Chore
- [ ] Breaking change

## Summary

<!-- What changed and why? Keep it concise and outcome-oriented. -->

## Problem / Context

<!-- What learning gap, navigation problem, or documentation weakness does this PR address? -->

## Changes Made

- 
- 
- 

## Areas Changed

- `README.md`, `paths/`, or topic `README.md` entrypoints: why the navigation or teaching flow changed
- `topics/...`: which docs were deepened and what learning pattern was improved
- `docs/...` or `.github/...`: any supporting process or review material added for this repo

## How to Review

1. Read the summary and problem/context first.
2. Check whether the changed docs explain jargon early and keep the smallest useful mental model visible.
3. Review whether examples, weak-vs-better contrasts, and tradeoffs are present where the topic teaches judgment.

## Validation Evidence

- Markdown links checked locally
- No runnable code changes

## Risks / Trade-offs

- Documentation may become slightly longer when mental models and contrasts are made explicit
- Some wording changes may still need taste-level review for tone or redundancy

## Backward Compatibility

- [x] No breaking changes
- [ ] Breaking changes (described below)

## Deployment / Rollout Notes

- No deployment impact
- No runtime or config changes

## Checklist

- [ ] Scope is focused and aligned with the issue
- [ ] Documentation keeps the repo's teaching style consistent
- [ ] Jargon is explained where first used
- [ ] Tradeoffs or failure modes are visible where they matter
- [ ] Local verification completed
